title: linaria × sepium var. camusii
intro: Linaria × sepium var. camusii, also known as Camus' Toadflax, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the larger Linaria × sepium species, a type of flowering plant in the figwort family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Camus' Toadflax include its ability to relieve congestion, reduce fever, and soothe irritated mucous membranes, making it a valuable remedy for respiratory health. In herbal preparations, Camus' Toadflax is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
